user_query:
I’m setting up a daily review routine for incoming clinical study proposals. Please create a small, reproducible command-line pipeline that I can schedule daily, using only the local files in this workspace. Use input/proposals.csv and config/schedule.yaml. Implement a runnable script at ./run_review_once.sh that, when executed, does all of the following:

1) Filters and prioritizes proposals: consider only rows where status is either "new" or "resubmitted"; sort by risk_score ascending (lower is better), breaking ties by sample_size descending, then by id ascending. Write outputs/review_queue.csv sorted exactly in that order with these columns: id,title,pi,status,risk_score,sample_size,method,population,rank,ethics_flag. Set rank starting at 1 in the final order. Set ethics_flag to true if population == "pediatric" OR risk_score >= 7, else false.

2) Rewrites feedback notes: for each proposal included in the review queue, read its draft_feedback text and produce outputs/feedback/{id}.md in a professional, neutral tone. Each file should contain: one-sentence overall assessment (<= 25 words) followed by exactly three bullet points focused on (a) ethics/safety if relevant, (b) methodological clarity/power, and (c) outcomes/data management. Keep the entire note under 120 words, avoid second-person pronouns, and remove colloquialisms.

3) Summary report: write outputs/summary.md that includes: (a) total number of proposals in input/proposals.csv, (b) number considered (status in {new,resubmitted}), (c) number with ethics_flag == true, (d) a Top-3 list with rank,id,title in order, and (e) a line "next_run_schedule: <frequency and time>" that reflects config/schedule.yaml, e.g., "daily at 09:00 local".

4) Scheduling preview and logging: create artifacts/cron_preview.txt containing a single cron line that would run the script daily at the time specified in config/schedule.yaml, redirecting stdout/stderr to logs/last_run.log (do not install the cron job; only write the preview). Ensure ./run_review_once.sh appends a timestamped entry to logs/last_run.log when it runs.

Finally, execute ./run_review_once.sh now so the outputs and logs exist. Do not modify the input files. The behavior should be fully deterministic from the provided inputs, and the deliverables must be created at: outputs/review_queue.csv, outputs/feedback/*.md, outputs/summary.md, artifacts/cron_preview.txt, and logs/last_run.log. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
